phase1_analyze.summary: Suite 36 contains 133 cases (CFG-PDP-001 to CFG-E2E-051) in the enriched 15-column agent-native format. The suite is broadly comprehensive but has six gap areas: (1) BL-CFG-001 through BL-CFG-004 are referenced 105+ times but do NOT exist in business-logic.md — these are phantom invariant IDs; the correct applicable IDs are BL-CAT-006, BL-PRICE-001/003, BL-CART-007, BL-CROSS-005. (2) ECL-14.1 is referenced 54 times and ECL-14.5 (Configurable Product & Variation Edge Cases) is NEVER referenced, despite directly describing this domain. (3) The changeCartConfiguredItem mutation (documented in xAPI) has no dedicated negative/error test cases. (4) GA4 tracking events (view_item, add_to_cart, purchase) for configured products are entirely absent. (5) BL-CROSS-009 (eventual consistency 120s window) is not tested for config section changes. (6) Admin-side cases CFG-ADM-001–CFG-ADM-003 have empty Business_Rule fields. Additionally, CFG-GQL-004 references optionIds field in addItem mutation but the xAPI documentation shows configurationSections with sectionId+productId+quantity structure — this is a schema mismatch.
phase1_analyze.formatAssessment: ENRICHED 15-column (agent-native). Format is correct. No legacy format issues.

phantomIDs.note: These IDs do not exist in .claude/agents/knowledge/business-logic.md. The BL-CFG domain is not defined. The closest valid invariants are BL-CAT-006 (configurable product requires all sections filled), BL-CAT-001 (stock zero disables purchase), BL-PRICE-001 (discount stacking), BL-CROSS-005 (order placement side effects). All BL-CFG-* references must be replaced.

suggestedMapping.BL-CFG-001: BL-CAT-006 (configurable sections must be completed before add-to-cart) + BL-PRICE-001 (price stacking on option selection)
suggestedMapping.BL-CFG-002: BL-CAT-006 (quantity stepper enables add-to-cart only when all required sections filled)
suggestedMapping.BL-CFG-003: BL-PRICE-001 + BL-PRICE-003 (option price calculation, sale/list price display)
suggestedMapping.BL-CFG-004: BL-CART-007 (same configured product creates separate lines if different configuration)

uncoveredInvariantsForDomain: BL-CROSS-009 (eventual consistency 120s) — not tested for config section changes propagating to storefront
BL-CROSS-012 ($0 product safety) — not tested for configured product price deletion edge case
BL-CAT-003 (search index lag 30-60s) — referenced in E2E admin-edit cases but no dedicated timing verification case
BL-B2B-007 (org-specific pricing on options) — only partially covered by CFG-B2B-003

missingHighValuePatterns.description: Section 14.5 directly covers: Add to Cart enabled before selection, VirtoFrontend_UI_Layout property absent, Image not switching on variant select, Unavailable combo not blocked. None of the 133 cases reference ECL-14.5.

missingHighValuePatterns.pattern: Loading States & Race Conditions — Double-click Place Order
missingHighValuePatterns.description: CFG-E2E-037 to 039 cover admin concurrency but not user double-click on add-to-cart for configured products specifically.

missingHighValuePatterns.pattern: Session & Timeout — session timeout during configuration
missingHighValuePatterns.description: User partially configures product (fills 2 of 3 sections), session expires — no case covers this specific scenario for configurable products.

coverageGaps.description: BL-CFG-001 through BL-CFG-004 are phantom invariant IDs referenced in 145 places across the suite. These IDs do not exist in business-logic.md. This creates false traceability — auditors cannot verify coverage claims against the authoritative rule set.
coverageGaps.affectedCases: ALL cases with BL-CFG-* in Business_Rule column (105+ cases)
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — false coverage reporting; cannot confirm BL coverage in RTM
coverageGaps.action: FIX — replace all BL-CFG-* references with valid BL-* IDs per the mapping table above

coverageGaps.description: The changeCartConfiguredItem mutation (documented in xAPI as the canonical way to update a configured line item) appears in CFG-VAR-015 and CFG-GQL-006/007 but has no negative test cases: (a) invalid sectionId, (b) expired/deleted option, (c) missing required section after change, (d) quantity set to 0 via mutation. CFG-GQL-008 covers addItem errors but not changeCartConfiguredItem errors.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — mutation errors silent in xAPI (ECL-14.1 pattern); invalid config could reach checkout
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 2 new cases: changeCartConfiguredItem with invalid sectionId; changeCartConfiguredItem sets required section to null

coverageGaps.description: Zero GA4 tracking cases exist in Suite 36. Configurable products have unique GA4 event requirements: view_item should include configuration_sections property; add_to_cart should include configured item details and total value (base + option surcharges); purchase event should reflect configured total. GA4 is a revenue-reporting dependency (BL-CROSS-005 side effect #3).
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P2 — revenue analytics inaccuracy; configured product purchases not correctly attributed
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 3 new P2 cases: view_item on configurable PDP; add_to_cart with option surcharge in value; purchase event for configured product

coverageGaps.description: BL-CROSS-009 (120s consistency window) is not explicitly tested for configuration section changes. CFG-E2E-013 (edit option price) and CFG-E2E-014 (delete option) rely on reindex but neither verifies the 120s window or detects if stale config persists beyond that. CFG-ADM-005 mentions 30-60s wait but does not assert that after 120s the change is guaranteed visible.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P2 — users may see stale option prices in the 30-120s window; not blocking but worth explicit coverage
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 1 new timing verification case with explicit 120s wait and dual assertion (stale before / correct after)

coverageGaps.description: CFG-ADM-001, CFG-ADM-002, and CFG-ADM-003 have empty Business_Rule fields (column 5 blank). These are High priority cases. Per review criterion BL-001, High cases must have Business_Rule entries.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — missing traceability for admin configuration creation path
coverageGaps.action: FIX — add BL-CAT-006 to Business_Rule for CFG-ADM-001, CFG-ADM-002, CFG-ADM-003

coverageGaps.description: CFG-GQL-004 (addItem with Product Section) uses optionIds field in the mutation: configurationSections: [{ sectionId: ..., optionIds: [...] }]. The xAPI documentation (changeCartConfiguredItem) shows the value object uses productId + quantity, not optionIds. The addItem mutation documentation does not expose configurationSections parameter — it is likely a frontend-abstracted flow. The test step may be testing an incorrect schema.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — test may be testing wrong schema and passing/failing for wrong reasons
coverageGaps.action: MANUAL — requires human verification of actual addItem schema against introspection to confirm correct parameter names

coverageGaps.description: CFG-MOB-001, CFG-MOB-002, and CFG-MOB-003 have empty Business_Rule fields. These are High/Medium priority cases covering the mobile purchase flow.
coverageGaps.action: FIX — add BL-CAT-006 to Business_Rule for CFG-MOB-001, CFG-MOB-002, CFG-MOB-003

phase2_generate.summary: Generated 6 new test cases addressing the highest-priority gaps: 2 for changeCartConfiguredItem negative paths (GAP-S36-002, P1), 3 for GA4 tracking (GAP-S36-003, P2), and 1 for eventual consistency timing (GAP-S36-004, P2). The phantom BL-CFG-* references (GAP-S36-001) and empty Business_Rule fields (GAP-S36-005, 007) are handled in Phase 4 as auto-fixes, not new cases. GAP-S36-006 (schema accuracy) is escalated to manual review.

newCases.title: changeCartConfiguredItem with Invalid sectionId Returns errors[] Non-Empty
newCases.section: Configurable Products > GraphQL xAPI > Error Handling
newCases.preconditions: User authenticated. A configured product is in the cart. lineItemId and a valid configurationSections structure known from previous addItem.
newCases.steps: [AUTH] Obtain Bearer token via POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}}; [SETUP] Ensure a configured product is in cart — record lineItemId; [GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with mutation changeCartConfiguredItem(command: { storeId: {{STORE_ID}} userId: <user-id> lineItemId: <lineItemId> configurationSections: [{ sectionId: "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000" value: { productId: "any-id" quantity: 1 } }] quantity: 1 }) { items { configurationItems { sectionId } } errors { code description } }; [VAR] Capture errors[] array from response
newCases.assertions: [ERRORS] errors[] is NON-EMPTY — mutation rejected invalid sectionId; [DATA] errors[].code is present and non-null; [STATE] Cart items remain unchanged (original configurationItems preserved); [DATA] HTTP status is 200 (xAPI convention) but errors[] non-empty signals rejection
newCases.crossLayerChecks: [ROUNDTRIP] Follow-up cart query confirms original configurationItems unchanged; [CONSOLE] No unhandled JS errors if tested via storefront; [NETWORK] HTTP 200 with non-empty errors[]
newCases.failureSignals: errors[] is empty despite invalid sectionId — silent acceptance of bad data; cart configurationItems changed to reflect invalid section; HTTP 4xx/5xx instead of 200+errors[]
newCases.cleanup: No cleanup needed — mutation rejected, cart unchanged

newCases.title: changeCartConfiguredItem Removing Required Section Selection Returns Validation Error
newCases.section: Configurable Products > GraphQL xAPI > Error Handling
newCases.preconditions: User authenticated. A configured product with a required Product section is in the cart — required section has a valid option selected. lineItemId and sectionId known.
newCases.steps: [AUTH] Obtain Bearer token via POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}}; [SETUP] Ensure configured product with required section in cart — record lineItemId and required sectionId; [GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with mutation changeCartConfiguredItem(command: { storeId: {{STORE_ID}} userId: <user-id> lineItemId: <lineItemId> configurationSections: [] quantity: 1 }) — pass empty configurationSections to attempt clearing required section; [VAR] Capture errors[] and items[].configurationItems from response
newCases.assertions: [ERRORS] errors[] is NON-EMPTY OR configurationItems still contains required section — system either rejects empty config or preserves required selection; [STATE] Required section selection not cleared if isRequired=true; [DATA] Response does not indicate checkout-ready state with missing required section
newCases.crossLayerChecks: [ROUNDTRIP] cart query confirms required configurationItem still present; [STOREFRONT] Cart page still shows required section as filled
newCases.failureSignals: errors[] is empty AND configurationItems cleared — required section silently removed enabling purchase without required config; cart proceeds to checkout with empty required section

newCases.title: GA4 view_item Event Fires on Configurable Product PDP
newCases.preconditions: Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}. GA4 / dataLayer accessible via browser console. Bike with options product at /products-with-options/configurations/build-the-bike-of-your-dreams/bike-with-options.
newCases.steps: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurations/build-the-bike-of-your-dreams/bike-with-options; [WAIT] Page fully loaded and configuration widget visible; [KEY] Execute in browser console: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'view_item'); [VAR] Capture view_item event data from dataLayer
newCases.assertions: [STATE] dataLayer contains exactly one view_item event after page load; [DATA] view_item.ecommerce.items[0].item_id matches product SKU CVQ-54616437 or product UUID; [DATA] view_item.ecommerce.items[0].item_name = 'Bike with options' (or equivalent); [DATA] view_item.ecommerce.value matches base product price $350.00; [DATA] view_item.ecommerce.currency = 'USD' (or active currency)
newCases.crossLayerChecks: [NETWORK] Google Analytics collection endpoint receives view_item payload; [CONSOLE] No GA4 related JS errors
newCases.failureSignals: No view_item in dataLayer; view_item fires before page load completes; wrong product ID; value = 0 instead of base price; event duplicated (fires twice on single page load)

newCases.title: GA4 add_to_cart Event Includes Configured Total Value (Base + Option Surcharge)
newCases.preconditions: Storefront accessible. User authenticated. Bike with options PDP loaded. Cart empty. dataLayer accessible.
newCases.steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP; [WAIT] Configure widget visible; [ACT] Select Seat option (total $365.00); [ACT] Set qty to 1; [ACT] Click 'Add to Cart'; [WAIT] Success notification; [KEY] Execute in browser console: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'add_to_cart'); [VAR] Capture add_to_cart event from dataLayer
newCases.assertions: [STATE] dataLayer contains add_to_cart event after button click; [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.value = 365.00 (base $350 + Seat option $15) — NOT $350 (base only); [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.items[0].price = 365.00; [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.items[0].quantity = 1; [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.currency = 'USD'
newCases.crossLayerChecks: [NETWORK] GA4 collection endpoint receives add_to_cart with value=365.00; [CONSOLE] No GA4 JS errors; [API] addItem mutation returns 200 with cart item price matching 365.00
newCases.failureSignals: add_to_cart event missing from dataLayer; value = 350.00 (base only, ignoring option surcharge — revenue underreported); value = 0; event fires before add-to-cart action completes

newCases.title: GA4 purchase Event for Configured Product Reports Configured Total Correctly
newCases.preconditions: User authenticated with valid payment method. Bike with options PDP accessible. Cart empty. GA4 dataLayer accessible.
newCases.testData: user={{USER_EMAIL}}; password={{USER_PASSWORD}}; front_url={{FRONT_URL}}; seat_total=365.00; card={{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}}; exp={{TEST_CARD_EXP}}; cvv={{TEST_CARD_CVV}}
newCases.steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP; [WAIT] Configure widget; [ACT] Select Seat option (total $365.00); [ACT] Set qty 1; [ACT] Click 'Add to Cart'; [WAIT] Success notification; [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; [ACT] Proceed to checkout — complete shipping address and payment; [ACT] Click 'Place Order'; [WAIT] Order confirmation page loads; [KEY] Execute in browser console: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'purchase'); [VAR] Capture purchase event from dataLayer
newCases.assertions: [STATE] dataLayer contains purchase event after order confirmation page loads; [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.value = 365.00 (configured total including option); [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.transaction_id matches order number on confirmation page; [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.items[0].price = 365.00; [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.items[0].quantity = 1; [DATA] purchase event fires exactly once (not on confirmation page refresh)
newCases.crossLayerChecks: [NETWORK] GA4 collection endpoint receives purchase payload; [CONSOLE] No GA4 JS errors; [ADMIN] Order exists in admin with matching total; [API] Order total = 365.00 + shipping + tax
newCases.failureSignals: purchase event missing; value = 350.00 (base only — option surcharge lost in analytics); transaction_id null or mismatched; purchase fires twice on confirmation page refresh; value includes tax when it should be pre-tax revenue
newCases.cleanup: Order placed — cancel via admin if needed for re-run

newCases.title: E2E: Eventual Consistency — Config Section Price Change Reflected on Storefront Within 120 Seconds
newCases.preconditions: Admin logged in at {{BACK_URL}}. Configurable Hat product exists with a Product section option. Storefront PDP for Configurable Hat accessible. Initial option price known.
newCases.testData: admin={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}; admin_pass={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}; back_url={{BACK_URL}}; front_url={{FRONT_URL}}; product_url=/products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/configurable-hat; initial_option_price=<recorded before test>; new_option_price=<initial + $5>
newCases.steps: [NAV] In admin: navigate to {{BACK_URL}} > Catalog > Products > Configurable Hat > Configuration tab; [ASSERT] Record current price of the first Product section option (initial_option_price); [ACT] Edit the option price to initial_option_price + $5.00; [SAVE] Save the configuration change; [WAIT] Note exact timestamp T0 of save; [NAV] Immediately navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/configurable-hat in storefront (same or new browser tab); [ASSERT] Check if option price already updated — may still show old price within first 60s (expected per BL-CAT-003); [WAIT] 120 seconds from T0; [NAV] Hard reload the storefront PDP; [ACT] Select the edited option; [ASSERT] Observe total price
newCases.assertions: [STATE] Within first 60s: old price may still be shown on storefront (acceptable — within lag window per BL-CAT-003); [STATE] At T0+120s (2 reindex cycles): storefront MUST show new updated option price — old price after 120s is a BL-CROSS-009 violation; [MATH] Total price after option selection = base price + new_option_price; [FORMAT] Price formatted to 2 decimal places
newCases.crossLayerChecks: [API] productConfiguration GraphQL query at T0+120s returns updated salePrice for the edited option; [ADMIN] Configuration blade still shows new option price (admin always live); [CONSOLE] No errors on price display
newCases.failureSignals: Storefront still shows old option price after 120s; total price calculation uses stale price; productConfiguration GraphQL response cached beyond 120s; admin save fails silently
newCases.cleanup: Restore option price to initial_option_price after test

phase3_review.summary: Static review of all 133 existing cases across 7 dimensions. Major systemic finding: BL-CFG-* phantom IDs (Dim 6, affects 100+ cases). Schema accuracy issue in CFG-GQL-004 (Dim 5). Missing ECL-14.5 references across all ECL-14 related patterns (Dim 6). Several cases have [ASSERT] steps used as final verdict assertions inside Steps column (Dim 2). Most cases are well-structured with good use of tags, preconditions, and test data bindings.

dimensionScores.D1_structure: PASS — 15-column header correct; all IDs present; no duplicate IDs detected; priorities use valid values
dimensionScores.D2_determinism: MOSTLY PASS — step tags present throughout; specific UI labels used (e.g., 'Add to Cart', 'Bike with options'). Issue: Several cases use [ASSERT] in Steps column for final verdicts that belong in Assertions (D-006). Examples: CFG-PDP-001 Steps column ends with [ASSERT] statements that duplicate the Assertions column.
dimensionScores.D3_completeness: PASS — preconditions present for all cases; Cross_Layer_Checks populated; Failure_Signals present. Minor: CFG-ADM-001/002/003 Business_Rule is empty (High cases, should have BL reference per C-001 equivalent).
dimensionScores.D4_testability: MOSTLY PASS — assertions are specific with formulas in [MATH] assertions. Issue in CFG-E2E-049 assertion 'matches Admin configuration order' could be more specific about what field defines order.
dimensionScores.D5_dataValidity: MOSTLY PASS — {{VAR}} bindings present in Test_Data. Issue: CFG-GQL-004 uses 'optionIds' parameter in the addItem mutation steps which does not match xAPI documented schema (changeCartConfiguredItem uses productId+quantity, not optionIds). This is a data validity / schema accuracy issue.
dimensionScores.D6_blEclCoverage: CRITICAL FAIL — BL-CFG-001 through BL-CFG-004 are phantom IDs not defined in business-logic.md. Referenced 145 times across suite. ECL-14.5 (the most specific ECL section for this domain) is never referenced.
dimensionScores.D7_duplication: MOSTLY PASS — the suite has logical layering and each case tests a distinct scenario. CFG-E2E-012 (quantity update recalculates) and CFG-PDP-005 (quantity stepper) have minor step overlap but test different contexts (cart vs PDP) so not a true duplicate.

reviewFindings.issue: Business_Rule references BL-CFG-001 / BL-CFG-002 / BL-CFG-003 / BL-CFG-004 which do not exist in business-logic.md. These phantom IDs create false traceability and fail BL-002 (invalid BL-* reference).
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: Replace all occurrences: BL-CFG-001 → BL-CAT-006; BL-CFG-002 → BL-CAT-006; BL-CFG-003 → BL-PRICE-001; BL-CFG-003 (where also covers price display) → BL-PRICE-003; BL-CFG-004 → BL-CART-007. Detailed mapping in phase1_analyze.invariantAudit.

reviewFindings.issue: Steps use optionIds as parameter name in addItem mutation configurationSections. xAPI documentation for changeCartConfiguredItem shows value.productId + value.quantity, not optionIds. The addItem mutation docs do not expose configurationSections at all — this may be a frontend-abstracted xCart API parameter name mismatch.
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: MANUAL — run GraphQL introspection against {{BACK_URL}}/graphql to verify InputAddItemType includes configurationSections field and confirm exact parameter names. Update steps to match verified schema.

reviewFindings.issue: Business_Rule column is empty for a High priority case. Per BL-001, High cases must have a BL-* reference.
Business_Rule column is empty for a High priority case.
Business_Rule column is empty for a High priority case.
Business_Rule column is empty for a High priority mobile case.
Business_Rule column is empty for a Medium priority mobile case.
Business_Rule column is empty for a High priority mobile case.

Assertion 'Section B precedes section A in the list after re-opening blade' is slightly ambiguous — does not specify the UI element (blade list row position) or what attribute confirms order. T-002 style issue.
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: Make assertion more specific: '[BLADE] Configuration section list: row 1 shows Section B name; row 2 shows Section A name'

reviewFindings.issue: Steps column contains multiple [ASSERT] tags as final-verdict assertions (e.g., '[ASSERT] Verify 5 radio options visible', '[ASSERT] Verify None is selected by default') that duplicate entries in the Assertions column. D-006: final-verdict assertions should live in Assertions column, not Steps.
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: Retain mid-flow [ASSERT] steps that gate subsequent actions; move final-verdict [ASSERT] steps to Assertions column as [DOM]/[STATE] assertions. This is a style issue and does not block execution — low priority.

reviewFindings.issue: Step '[ACT] Attempt to enter negative quantity in field if editable' is conditional ('if editable') which introduces ambiguity. An agent may skip this step and still pass.
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: Convert to two separate cases: one for editable field, one for stepper-only. Or make deterministic: '[ACT] If quantity input is directly editable, type -1; [ASSERT] input rejects or shows 0'

reviewFindings.issue: References ECL-14.1 but the case is about predefined dictionary text values — ECL-14.5 (Configurable Product Edge Cases) is more directly applicable.
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: Add ECL-14.5 to Edge_Case_Refs alongside ECL-14.1

reviewFindings.issue: Same as CFG-TEXT-001 — ECL-14.5 not referenced for a case testing configurable text section behavior.
Security test (XSS attempt in text field) correctly references Critical priority but ECL-14.5 edge case about text field input security not referenced.

Price integrity test (DOM manipulation cannot override server price) — BL-CFG-003 reference should be BL-PRICE-001 (correct invariant for price integrity is BL-PRICE-001 and BL-CROSS-012, not the phantom BL-CFG-003). Also missing ECL-14.5.
reviewFindings.suggestedFix: Replace BL-CFG-003 with BL-PRICE-001; BL-CROSS-012; add ECL-14.5 to Edge_Case_Refs

phase4_fix.summary: Auto-fixes applied: (1) BL-CFG-* → valid BL-* replacements for all 133 existing cases using the mapping table. (2) BL-CAT-006 added to 6 cases with empty Business_Rule (CFG-ADM-001/002/003, CFG-MOB-001/002/003). (3) ECL-14.5 added to Edge_Case_Refs for CFG-TEXT-001, CFG-TEXT-002, CFG-E2E-027. (4) BL-PRICE-001; BL-CROSS-012 substituted for BL-CFG-003 in CFG-E2E-034. Manual items: CFG-GQL-004 schema verification, CFG-PDP-005 step ambiguity, CFG-E2E-049 assertion specificity.
